phase_name: v1.3.0 CLOSED + post-close web reconciliation, blog, and catalog-sync 2h default
milestone_context: v1.3.0 milestone was CLOSED & ARCHIVED earlier this session (tag v1.3.0 local; beekeeper repo unpushed). Everything after that was ad-hoc maintainer-requested improvements, all committed and fully gated. We are between milestones. Working tree is clean (only pre-existing untracked artifacts remain: .claude/, 16-PATTERNS.md, .understand-anything/, AGENTS.md, *.html prototypes).

completed_tasks.name: Closed v1.3.0 milestone BY HAND (archives + git rm REQUIREMENTS + consumed checkpoints + local tag v1.3.0; parked v1.1.0 Pollen preserved)

Audit-driven doc reconciliation: collapse changelog to single public v1.0.0, de-assert published claims, site badge v1.3.0->v1.0.0, document catalog daemon + Sentry 001-008 + DNS-ingested + Tier A/B/C validation, fix THREAT-MODEL DNS line + audit-rotation + harness counts
Add /blog section (3rd fumadocs collection, marketing-chrome article layout) + launch article

Rewrite launch article around the REAL threat model (supply-chain, drop hijacked-agent framing; progressive disclosure naming each system part; honest 'quarantine sound-by-design not exploit-proven' line)
Split the launch-article install commands into per-command cards (sequential/dependent, not simultaneous)
Catalog-sync default 12h -> 2h: lowered clamp floor 5h->2h so the default takes effect; updated config + config_test + TUI selector + docs + changelog + blog

remaining_tasks.name: Decide next milestone: run /gsd-new-milestone OR (independently) resume the parked v1.1.0 Pollen GSD close via docs/release-runbook.md
OPTIONAL deferred: apply the per-command card split to the DOCS pages (cli-reference, integration install lists) per .planning/todos/pending/docs-command-card-copy.md (done for the blog only)
OPTIONAL: SITE-03 live Vercel deploy of the v1.3.0 site (the one deferred requirement; needs repo push + Vercel setup)
OPTIONAL: cosign verify-blob the Pollen v0.2.0 release locally; surface v0.2.0 in install docs

human_actions_pending.action: Stop or keep the background preview server
human_actions_pending.context: A `serve out` static preview is running at http://localhost:3000 (background task id bgzxhc2ly). It persists across the pause; stop it with TaskStop if not needed.

human_actions_pending.action: Choose the next direction (new milestone vs resume parked Pollen vs deploy)
human_actions_pending.context: v1.3.0 is closed; no work is in flight. The canonical GSD next step is /gsd-new-milestone.

decisions.decision: Public release framing = a SINGLE v1.0.0 changelog entry (everything folded in); v1.2.0/v1.3.0 changelog pages deleted; site version badge = v1.0.0
decisions.rationale: Internal milestone tags are not public releases; the repo is unpushed so 'published/signed release' claims were de-asserted. Do NOT re-add v1.2/v1.3 changelog pages or bump the badge past v1.0.0.

decisions.decision: catalog-sync default lowered 12h -> 2h AND clamp floor 5h -> 2h
decisions.rationale: 12h is too coarse for threat-intel freshness. The floor had to drop too or the 2h default would be clamped back to 5h. Existing 12h configs stay valid (within [2h,24h]); ETag conditional requests keep 2h cadence cheap.

decisions.decision: Launch article reframed to supply-chain risk, not 'hijacked/misaligned agent'
decisions.rationale: Maintainer: agents rarely go rogue; the real, common risk is an agent faithfully installing a compromised package/extension. Quarantine + Sentry are honestly 'sound by design, not yet exploit-proven'.

next_action: Nothing is mid-flight; everything is committed and green. On resume, first decide the next direction (/gsd-new-milestone, or resume parked v1.1.0 Pollen). Optionally stop the background preview server (TaskStop bgzxhc2ly) and pick up the deferred docs command-card split.
context_notes: Long session that started as /gsd-resume-work -> /gsd-complete-milestone v1.3.0, then turned into a maintainer-driven polish pass on the web site (accuracy reconciliation, single-v1.0.0 release framing, a new /blog with the launch article, and the catalog-sync 2h default). Every change was committed atomically and fully gated each time (Go: build+vet+go test ./...; web: lint+typecheck+unit 33+build+postbuild SEO 29+e2e 13). The phase-number-resolver regression and the 'reconcile STATE by hand' caveats still apply. Reusable gotcha discovered this session: changing the catalog-sync default also requires lowering the clamp FLOOR (two paths: parseClampCatalogSyncInterval clamps; the TUI persistCatalogSyncInterval validates-and-rejects out-of-range), or the new default is silently clamped.
